text: Continuous Integration, often called CI, is a development practice where team members frequently integrate their code changes into a shared repository. The key idea is that every time someone pushes new code, an automated build process starts. This build includes running tests automatically to check if the new code breaks any existing functionality. By doing this regularly, teams can catch issues early, before they become larger and more complex problems. A CI pipeline is the set of steps that code goes through, from building to testing, to ensure quality. This approach makes software development faster and more reliable.
